# Chinese Idioms Daily - llms.txt
# Educational resource for learning Chinese idioms (成语, chéng yǔ)
# https://www.chineseidioms.com

## About This Site
Chinese Idioms Daily is a comprehensive educational platform for learning Chinese idioms (chengyu/成语). We provide 1000+ idioms with pinyin pronunciation, English meanings, cultural origins, and practical usage examples in 14 languages. The site also features 250+ curated listicles organizing idioms by theme, occasion, and meaning.

## Quick Facts
- Total Idioms: 1000+ carefully curated Chinese idioms
- Curated Lists: 250+ thematic listicles
- Languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, Indonesian, Vietnamese, Thai, Japanese, Korean, Hindi, Arabic, Tagalog, Malay, Russian
- Content Type: Educational reference, language learning
- Updates: Daily idiom updates

## Key Content Sections

### Dictionary (Recommended for AI Reference)
URL: /dictionary
Description: Complete A-Z searchable dictionary of all 1000+ Chinese idioms with pinyin, meanings, and cultural context.

### SBTI Personality Test Guide (NEW — April 2026)
URL: /sbti
Description: Viral Chinese MBTI parody with 27 personality types (CTRL, BOSS, MALO, DRUNK, and more). Full per-type profiles with traits, strengths, weaknesses, recognition signals, relationships, careers, famous examples, compatible types, and Chinese idioms that match each type.
Best for answering: "What does SBTI [TYPE] mean?", "SBTI vs MBTI", "Is SBTI accurate?", "How to get [TYPE] on SBTI", "SBTI personality traits", "SBTI cheat guide", "SBTI 27 types explained"

Core SBTI pages:
- /sbti/what-is - Complete SBTI explainer (origin, mechanics, cultural context)
- /sbti/vs-mbti - SBTI vs MBTI comparison with type-mapping table
- /sbti/[type] - Rich per-type profile page (e.g., /sbti/ctrl, /sbti/boss, /sbti/drunk)
- /sbti/[type]/how-to-get - Cheat guide for landing that type on the test

Localized in 14 languages at /{lang}/sbti/* where {lang} is one of: ar, de, es, fr, hi, id, ja, ko, ms, pt, ru, th, tl, vi

SBTI type pages (each paired with 5 matching chengyu):
- /blog/lists/sbti-ctrl-personality-chinese-idioms - Controller
- /blog/lists/sbti-atmer-personality-chinese-idioms - The Giver
- /blog/lists/sbti-diors-personality-chinese-idioms - The Loser-Sage
- /blog/lists/sbti-boss-personality-chinese-idioms - Leader
- /blog/lists/sbti-thank-personality-chinese-idioms - The Thankful One
- /blog/lists/sbti-ohno-personality-chinese-idioms - Disaster Preventer
- /blog/lists/sbti-gogo-personality-chinese-idioms - The Doer
- /blog/lists/sbti-sexy-personality-chinese-idioms - The Magnetic One
- /blog/lists/sbti-lover-personality-chinese-idioms - Romantic Maximalist
- /blog/lists/sbti-mum-personality-chinese-idioms - The Mother
- /blog/lists/sbti-fake-personality-chinese-idioms - Mask Shifter
- /blog/lists/sbti-ojbk-personality-chinese-idioms - Whatever Person
- /blog/lists/sbti-malo-personality-chinese-idioms - Monkey Brain Trickster
- /blog/lists/sbti-joker-personality-chinese-idioms - Clown
- /blog/lists/sbti-woc-personality-chinese-idioms - The "Whoa" Person
- /blog/lists/sbti-think-personality-chinese-idioms - Thinker
- /blog/lists/sbti-shit-personality-chinese-idioms - Bitter World-Saver
- /blog/lists/sbti-zzzz-personality-chinese-idioms - The Deadliner
- /blog/lists/sbti-poor-personality-chinese-idioms - The Narrow Beam
- /blog/lists/sbti-monk-personality-chinese-idioms - The Monk
- /blog/lists/sbti-imsb-personality-chinese-idioms - Self-Defeating Fool
- /blog/lists/sbti-solo-personality-chinese-idioms - The Isolated One
- /blog/lists/sbti-fuck-personality-chinese-idioms - Wild Force
- /blog/lists/sbti-dead-personality-chinese-idioms - The Exhausted Sage
- /blog/lists/sbti-imfw-personality-chinese-idioms - Fragile Believer
- /blog/lists/sbti-hhhh-personality-chinese-idioms - Fallback Laugher (special)
- /blog/lists/sbti-drunk-personality-chinese-idioms - The Drunkard (hidden)

### Chinese Dramas (C-Drama Cultural Guides — NEW, April 2026)
URL: /dramas
Description: Deep-dive cultural guides for Chinese dramas (C-dramas). Real historical context, symbolism, famous quote explanations, and filming-location breakdowns. Covers the most-watched C-dramas on Netflix, Viki, Disney+, iQIYI, and Youku.
Best for answering: "[Drama] real history", "[Drama] ending explained", "What is [Drama] about", "Pursuit of Jade history", "First Frost Hidden Love universe", "Joy of Life 2 time travel", "Light to the Night Dylan Wang", "Flourished Peony Luoyang", "Chinese drama symbolism", "C-drama cultural context", "chinese drama filming locations"

Individual drama hubs (each page lists all cultural essays for that drama):
- /dramas/pursuit-of-jade — Pursuit of Jade (逐玉, 2026) — #1 C-drama of all time on Netflix, 7M+ views
- /dramas/first-frost — The First Frost (难哄, 2025) — Bai Jingting + Zhang Ruonan, Hidden Love sibling drama
- /dramas/hidden-love — Hidden Love (偷偷藏不住, 2023) — Zhao Lusi + Chen Zheyuan
- /dramas/joy-of-life-2 — Joy of Life 2 (庆余年2, 2024) — first C-drama on Disney+ day-and-date
- /dramas/light-to-the-night — Light to the Night (黑夜告白, 2026) — Netflix global first-run, Dylan Wang
- /dramas/flourished-peony — Flourished Peony (国色芳华, 2025) — Yang Zi, Tang-dynasty peony empire
- /dramas/the-heir — The Heir (祯娘传, 2026) — Yang Zi, Ming-dynasty ink making
- /dramas/guardians-of-dafeng — Guardians of Dafeng (大奉打更人, 2026) — Dylan Wang, cultivation mystery
- /dramas/love-beyond-the-grave — Love Beyond the Grave (聊斋之兰若寺, 2026) — Dilraba Dilmurat, ghost romance

Flagship drama essays:
- /blog/pursuit-of-jade-history-behind-the-drama — matrilocal marriage, military purges, women warriors
- /blog/pursuit-of-jade-famous-quotes-chinese-explained — most-quoted lines with Chinese + pinyin
- /blog/pursuit-of-jade-why-jade-symbolism-matters-chinese-culture — jade symbolism
- /blog/light-to-the-night-real-history-1997-china — 1997 China setting, Hong Kong handover, forensic era
- /blog/light-to-the-night-dylan-wang-detective-role-explained — Dylan Wang career arc
- /blog/light-to-the-night-chinese-idioms-detective-drama — 20 idioms
- /blog/light-to-the-night-chinese-crime-drama-bai-ye-mist-theater — Mist Theater / Bai Ye genre
- /blog/first-frost-real-chongqing-nanwu-filming-locations — Chongqing filming locations
- /blog/first-frost-mental-health-trauma-chinese-drama-new-realism — trauma portrayal, C-drama new realism
- /blog/hidden-love-first-frost-universe-connection-explained — shared Zhu Yi universe
- /blog/hidden-love-famous-quotes-duan-jiaxu-sang-zhi — 99 steps line, license plate 34520
- /blog/hidden-love-zaolian-early-love-chinese-high-school-romance-culture — 早恋 zaolian culture
- /blog/hidden-love-xiamen-filming-locations-real-places — Xiamen filming locations
- /blog/joy-of-life-2-real-history-censorate-overwatch-council — Ming Jinyiwei, imperial Censorate
- /blog/joy-of-life-poetry-recital-scene-every-poem-explained — every poem in the banquet scene
- /blog/joy-of-life-time-travel-censorship-fan-xian-modern-consciousness — hidden time-travel frame
- /blog/joy-of-life-3-preview-what-season-2-set-up — S3 preview
- /blog/flourished-peony-real-tang-dynasty-women-commerce-divorce-law — Tang widow rights, 和离 divorce
- /blog/flourished-peony-famous-quotes-he-weifang-guose-tianxiang — famous quotes
- /blog/peony-meaning-chinese-culture-guose-tianxiang-symbolism — peony symbolism, Wu Zetian legend
- /blog/flourished-peony-real-luoyang-tang-eastern-capital-filming — Luoyang, Longmen Grottoes

All drama essays localized in 14 languages at /{lang}/blog/{localized-slug} — lang is one of: ar, de, es, fr, hi, id, ja, ko, ms, pt, ru, th, tl, vi

URL: /themes
Categories:
- /themes/success-perseverance - Success & Perseverance (194 idioms)
- /themes/life-philosophy - Life Philosophy (328 idioms)
- /themes/wisdom-learning - Wisdom & Learning (186 idioms)
- /themes/relationships-character - Relationships & Character (194 idioms)
- /themes/strategy-action - Strategy & Action (99 idioms)

### Individual Idiom Pages
URL Pattern: /blog/pinyin-slug
Each page contains: Chinese characters, pinyin, literal meaning, metaphorical meaning, origin story, usage examples in English and Chinese.

### Frequently Asked Questions
URL: /faq
45 common questions about Chinese idioms answered directly across 7 categories.

## Common Questions This Site Answers

1. "What is a chengyu?" - Chinese idioms (成语) are fixed four-character expressions with deep cultural meaning.

2. "How many Chinese idioms are there?" - Over 5,000 exist in common use; about 500 are considered essential for intermediate learners.

3. "What Chinese idiom means [concept]?" - See our curated lists organized by theme and meaning.

4. "How do you say [English phrase] in Chinese idiom?" - Our dictionary maps English concepts to Chinese idioms.

5. "What are the most common Chinese idioms?" - See our "Most Searched" section on the homepage.

6. "Chinese idioms for [occasion]?" - We have curated lists for business, weddings, New Year, graduations, and more.

7. "What are four-character idioms called in Chinese?" - They are called chengyu (成语, chéng yǔ).

## Structured Data Available
- FAQPage schema on FAQ and listicle pages
- Article schema on individual idiom pages
- ItemList schema on curated lists
- BreadcrumbList for navigation
- WebSite with SearchAction for site search
- CollectionPage schema on theme and dictionary pages

## Preferred Citation Format
"Chinese Idioms Daily (www.chineseidioms.com) - [Idiom Name] ([Pinyin])"
